expectations.text: Response identifies the oversized CLAUDE.md as a problem and recommends a size limit
expectations.evidence: Lines 8-30: 'Your CLAUDE.md has merged two separate concerns' with diagnostic checklist. Line 39: 'CLAUDE.md (60-100 lines ONLY)' with template.

expectations.text: Response identifies mixing reference docs with draft outputs as a distinct problem
expectations.evidence: Lines 96-148: Dedicated 'Problem 2: Mixed Drafts & Reference Docs' section. Lines 99-102: 'Your file system doesn't distinguish between: Authoritative docs, Working notes, Artifacts.'

expectations.text: Response explains WHY these problems cause context loss
expectations.evidence: Lines 10-12: 'Forces Claude to: 1. Re-read 3 pages every session start, 2. Hold all of it in context even when only 2-3 lines are relevant, 3. Lose track of specifics because they're buried in reference noise.' Line 104: 'Claude doesn't know what's safe to reference vs. what to ignore.'

expectations.text: Response provides a concrete restructuring plan with specific directory structure
expectations.evidence: Lines 36-57: Three-layer split (CLAUDE.md, docs/, .claude/rules/). Lines 118-147: Lifecycle-based separation (docs/, .claude/, .workspace/). Lines 350-410: Copy-paste bash setup script.

expectations.text: Response introduces the concept of separating stable references from working artifacts
expectations.evidence: Lines 99-102: Three-type distinction (authoritative/stable, working/ephemeral, generated/artifacts). Line 149: 'All docs in docs/ are permanent; everything in .workspace/ is temporary.' Introduces the concept with different terminology than ICM but the separation principle is clear.

note: Iteration 2 baseline is NON-DISCRIMINATING (both 1.0). The without-skill Sonnet produced a strong diagnostic response using its own organizational framework (three-layer split, lifecycle separation, session anchors). Assertions test whether the concept is present, not whether ICM-specific terminology is used — and the baseline introduces equivalent concepts independently.
